title: Where Will The Next Shortage Be?
gist: Louis-Vincent Gave argues capitalism's natural state is a deflationary boom, but a cascade of supply shocks since the 2018 US-China trade war has flipped the world into an inflationary regime, raising the question of where the next scarcity-driven price shock hits.

insights.claim: The natural resting state of capitalism is a deflationary boom, because every entrepreneur and business leader is constantly driven to produce more using fewer workers, less energy, land and commodity inputs.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Frames disinflation as the baseline, so persistent inflation should be read as evidence of a structural break driven by external shocks rather than as the norm to fade.
insights.quote: The natural state of capitalism is the deflationary boom; if only because every entrepreneur, CEO and business leader wakes up every morning wondering how to produce more with less

themes.theme: Serial supply shocks flipping the regime to inflation

insights.claim: The 2018 onset of the US-China trade war and the later semiconductor embargo were the first major signposts that a deflationary world had turned inflationary.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Pins the regime turn to a specific 2018 inflection, arguing the inflationary trend is structural and geopolitical rather than a transitory post-Covid artifact.
insights.quote: The 2018 onset of the US-China trade war and later semiconductor embargo were the first major signposts that our deflationary world had taken an inflationary turn
insights.locator: The 2018 onset of the US-China trade war

insights.claim: A sequence of shocks since 2018 has added inflationary fuel: Covid lockdowns and the fiscal and monetary response, the Russia-Ukraine war, China's weaponization of rare earths, the Trump tariffs, and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: The implication is that repeated supply chokepoints keep restoking inflation, so investors should watch the next scarcity or chokepoint for the following price shock rather than betting on a clean return to disinflation.
insights.quote: events—from the lockdowns and the fiscal and monetary responses to Covid, the Russia-Ukraine war, China’s weaponization of rare earths, the Trump tariffs and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz—have added inflationary fuel to the underlying fire
